title: Vi Vision Diagram Archetype (render-type)
description:
The DIAGRAM archetype — an instance of archetype.schema.json (render_kind='diagram'): a structured AUTHORED relational picture (flow · sequence · hierarchy). Tim's 'diagrams'. The RELATIONAL family (like graph) but AUTHORED relations — the relations are declared/composed, NOT resolved from the substrate (that's graph).

WHAT IT RENDERS: nodes + AUTHORED edges as a flow/sequence/hierarchy. Grounded in REAL usage — flows (the decision-flow present→explain→choose as a picture), sequences, org/structure diagrams, the pipeline stages, a process map. DNA renders; the resolver places.

THE EDGE BINDING (same as graph, LOCKED w/ DNA 2026-06-21): `edges` is an EXPLICIT slot — a field 'edges' (array of {from,to,kind?}) OR 'resolve:relations' host-resolved to that array before render. The renderer always receives resolved edges; never resolves from node data itself. (graph = resolved-from-substrate relations; diagram = authored relations; SAME binding shape.)

Mostly READ (a diagram shows structure); an optional take navigates/drills a node.

LAWS (inherited from archetype.schema): operator-legibility · DNA-token-built · resolver-placed · navigable-not-text · native dual-device.

nodes.description: The NODE source — the authored elements (each with legibility name/is).

edges.description: The AUTHORED-EDGE source (LOCKED w/ DNA): an explicit slot — field 'edges' (array of {from,to,kind?}) OR 'resolve:relations' host-resolved to that array before render. The authored relations (flow/sequence/hierarchy links).

flow.description: Optional: the flow/direction/ordering source (for a sequence or directed flow).
